words.definitionEn: to the same degree; in the same or in a similar way
words.exampleEn: Diet and exercise are equally important.

words.definitionEn: the act or a method of escaping from a place or an unpleasant or dangerous situation
words.exampleEn: I had a narrow escape (= I was lucky to have escaped).

words.definitionEn: to get away from a place where you have been kept as a prisoner or not allowed to leave
words.exampleEn: Two prisoners have escaped.

words.definitionEn: completely necessary; extremely important in a particular situation or for a particular activity
words.exampleEn: an essential part/feature of something

words.definitionEn: at the end of a period of time or a series of events
words.exampleEn: Our flight eventually left five hours late.

words.definitionEn: to consider or study an idea, a subject, etc. very carefully
words.exampleEn: examine something, This study sets out to examine in detail the possible effects of climate change.

words.definitionEn: used before you mention something that makes a statement not completely true
words.exampleEn: I didn't tell him anything except that I needed the money.

words.definitionEn: an act of giving something to somebody or doing something for somebody and receiving something in return
words.exampleEn: The exchange of prisoners took place this morning.

words.definitionEn: to give something to somebody and at the same time receive the same type of thing from them
words.exampleEn: exchange something, to exchange news/information

words.definitionEn: the state of feeling or showing happiness and enthusiasm
words.exampleEn: The news caused great excitement among her friends.

words.definitionEn: a collection of things, for example works of art, that are shown to the public
words.exampleEn: Have you seen the Picasso exhibition?

words.definitionEn: to become greater in size, number or importance; to make something greater in size, number or importance
words.exampleEn: Metals expand when they are heated.

words.definitionEn: that you think will happen
words.exampleEn: Double the expected number of people came to the meeting.

words.definitionEn: an organized journey with a particular purpose, especially to find out about a place that is not well known
words.exampleEn: to plan/lead/go on an expedition

words.definitionEn: to have a particular situation affect you or happen to you
words.exampleEn: Many people have never experienced these difficulties first-hand.

words.definitionEn: having knowledge or skill in a particular job or activity
words.exampleEn: an experienced player/teacher

words.definitionEn: to do a scientific experiment or experiments
words.exampleEn: After experimenting at Columbia University, he realized that fission was the key to releasing nuclear energy.

words.definitionEn: to burst (= break apart) or make something burst loudly and violently, causing damage
words.exampleEn: A second bomb exploded in a crowded market.

words.definitionEn: to travel to or around an area or a country in order to learn about it
words.exampleEn: As soon as we arrived on the island we were eager to explore.

words.definitionEn: the sudden violent bursting and loud noise of something such as a bomb exploding; the act of deliberately causing something to explode
words.exampleEn: a bomb/nuclear/gas explosion

words.definitionEn: the selling and transporting of goods to another country
words.exampleEn: a ban on the export of live cattle

words.definitionEn: to sell and send goods to another country
words.exampleEn: export (something), The islands export sugar and fruit.

words.definitionEn: in addition; more than is usual, expected or exists already
words.exampleEn: to pay/cost extra

words.definitionEn: a thing that is added to something that is not usual, standard or necessary and that costs more
words.exampleEn: The monthly fee is fixed and there are no hidden extras (= unexpected costs).

words.definitionEn: to be opposite somebody/something; to have your face or front pointing towards somebody/something or in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: face somebody/something, She turned and faced him.

words.definitionEn: that can be used instead of something else
words.exampleEn: The road was closed so we had to find an alternative route.

words.definitionEn: to some extent but not very
words.exampleEn: fairly simple/easy/straightforward

words.definitionEn: well known to you; often seen or heard and therefore easy to recognize
words.exampleEn: to look/sound/seem familiar

words.definitionEn: unusually complicated, often in an unnecessary way; intended to impress other people
words.exampleEn: a kitchen full of fancy gadgets

words.definitionEn: to want something or want to do something
words.exampleEn: fancy something, Fancy a drink?

words.definitionEn: at a greater distance away from you
words.exampleEn: I saw her on the far side of the road.

words.definitionEn: extremely interesting and attractive
words.exampleEn: a fascinating story

words.definitionEn: following a style that is popular at a particular time
words.exampleEn: fashionable clothes/ideas/styles

words.definitionEn: to close or join together the two parts of something; to become closed or joined together
words.exampleEn: fasten something, Fasten your seat belts, please.

words.definitionEn: a thing that you do to help somebody
words.exampleEn: Could you do me a favour and pick up Sam from school today?

words.definitionEn: to be frightened of somebody/something or frightened of doing something
words.exampleEn: fear somebody/something, All his employees fear him.

words.definitionEn: to include a particular person or thing as a special feature
words.exampleEn: feature somebody/something as somebody/something, The film features Cary Grant as a professor.

words.definitionEn: a structure made of wood or wire supported with posts that is put between two areas of land as a boundary, or around a garden, field, etc. to keep animals in, or to keep people and animals out
words.exampleEn: The two women chatted over the garden fence.

words.definitionEn: the activity of being involved in a battle against an enemy
words.exampleEn: Fighting broke out in three districts of the city last night.

words.definitionEn: very surprised
words.exampleEn: an amazed look

words.definitionEn: a box or folded piece of card for keeping loose papers together and in order
words.exampleEn: a box file

words.definitionEn: connected with money and finance
words.exampleEn: the world's major financial markets/institutions

words.definitionEn: connected with education, especially studying in schools and universities
words.exampleEn: high/low academic standards

words.definitionEn: to shoot bullets, etc. from a gun or other weapon
words.exampleEn: The officer ordered his men to fire.

words.definitionEn: something that you want to do or achieve very much
words.exampleEn: She had fulfilled her lifelong ambition.

words.definitionEn: determined to be successful, rich, powerful, etc.
words.exampleEn: a fiercely ambitious young manager

words.definitionEn: the state of being physically healthy and strong
words.exampleEn: a magazine on health and fitness

words.definitionEn: staying the same; not changing or able to be changed
words.exampleEn: These fixed prices give farmers a degree of financial security.

words.definitionEn: a piece of cloth with a special coloured design on it that may be the symbol of a particular country or organization, may be used to give a signal or may have a particular meaning. A flag can be attached to a pole (= a long thin straight piece of wood or metal) or held in the hand.
words.exampleEn: the Italian flag

words.definitionEn: a large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry
words.exampleEn: The heavy rain has caused floods in many parts of the country.

words.definitionEn: if a place floods or something floods it, it becomes filled or covered with water
words.exampleEn: The cellar floods whenever it rains heavily.

words.definitionEn: a fine white or brown powder made from grain, especially wheat, and used in cooking for making bread, cakes, etc.
words.exampleEn: Sift the flour and salt into a bowl.

words.definitionEn: the steady and continuous movement of something/somebody in one direction
words.exampleEn: the flow of an electric current

words.definitionEn: to move steadily and continuously in one direction
words.exampleEn: She lost control and the tears began to flow.

words.definitionEn: to bend something, especially paper or cloth, so that one part lies on top of another part
words.exampleEn: fold something, First, fold the paper in half/in two.

words.definitionEn: traditional and typical of the ordinary people of a country or community
words.exampleEn: We visited an exhibition of folk art.

words.definitionEn: people in general
words.exampleEn: ordinary working-class folk

words.definitionEn: the thing or things that you will mention next; the person or people that you will mention next
words.exampleEn: The following is a summary of events.

words.definitionEn: violent physical action used to obtain or achieve something
words.exampleEn: The release of the hostages could not be achieved without the use of force.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody do something that they do not want to do
words.exampleEn: be forced into doing something, The President was forced into resigning.

words.definitionEn: used to say that a particular situation or state will always exist
words.exampleEn: I'll love you forever!

words.definitionEn: a strong border or structure of wood, metal, etc. that holds a picture, door, piece of glass, etc. in position
words.exampleEn: a picture/photo frame

words.definitionEn: to put or make a frame or border around something
words.exampleEn: be framed, The photograph had been framed.

words.definitionEn: to examine the nature or structure of something, especially by separating it into its parts, in order to understand or explain it
words.exampleEn: analyse something, The job involves collecting and analysing data.

words.definitionEn: to become hard, and often turn to ice, as a result of extreme cold; to make something do this
words.exampleEn: Water freezes at 0°C.

words.definitionEn: the detailed study or examination of something in order to understand more about it; the result of the study
words.exampleEn: statistical/data analysis

words.definitionEn: often
words.exampleEn: Buses run frequently between the city and the airport.

words.definitionEn: a relationship between friends
words.exampleEn: They formed a close friendship at college.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody suddenly feel afraid
words.exampleEn: frighten (somebody), Sorry, I didn't mean to frighten you.

words.definitionEn: afraid; feeling fear
words.exampleEn: a frightened child

words.definitionEn: making you feel afraid
words.exampleEn: a frightening experience/prospect/thought

words.definitionEn: kept at a very low temperature in order to preserve it
words.exampleEn: frozen peas/fish/pizza

words.definitionEn: to cook something in hot fat or oil; to be cooked in hot fat or oil
words.exampleEn: fry (something), fried fish

words.definitionEn: any material that produces heat or power, usually when it is burnt
words.exampleEn: diesel/jet/rocket fuel

words.definitionEn: a special activity or purpose of a person or thing
words.exampleEn: The club serves a useful function as a meeting place.

words.definitionEn: the soft thick mass of hair that grows on the body of some animals
words.exampleEn: The cat carefully licked its fur.

words.definitionEn: at or to a greater distance
words.exampleEn: We had walked a bit further than I had realized.

words.definitionEn: a building for keeping one or more cars or other vehicles in
words.exampleEn: a double garage (= one for two cars)

words.definitionEn: to come together, or bring people together, in one place to form a group
words.exampleEn: A crowd soon gathered.

words.definitionEn: by or to most people
words.exampleEn: The initiative was generally considered a success.

words.definitionEn: all the people who were born at about the same time
words.exampleEn: the younger/older generation

words.definitionEn: giving or willing to give freely; given freely
words.exampleEn: The gallery was named after its most generous benefactor.

words.definitionEn: calm and kind; doing things in a quiet and careful way
words.exampleEn: a kind and gentle man

words.definitionEn: a man who is polite and well educated, who has excellent manners and always behaves well
words.exampleEn: You acted like a true gentleman.

words.definitionEn: the spirit of a dead person that a living person believes they can see or hear
words.exampleEn: Do you believe in ghosts (= believe that they exist)?

words.definitionEn: very large; much larger or more important than similar things usually are
words.exampleEn: The match was shown on a giant screen outside the town hall.

words.definitionEn: (in stories) a very large strong person who is often cruel and stupid
words.exampleEn: tales of giants and ogres

words.definitionEn: pleased; happy
words.exampleEn: ‘I passed the test!’ ‘I’m so glad.’

words.definitionEn: covering or affecting the whole world
words.exampleEn: This year the global economy will grow by about 4 per cent.

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing for the hand, made of wool, leather, etc. with separate parts for each finger and the thumb
words.exampleEn: a pair of gloves

words.definitionEn: a person’s turn to move or play in a game or an activity
words.exampleEn: Whose go is it?

words.definitionEn: things that are produced to be sold
words.exampleEn: to produce/buy/sell goods

words.definitionEn: a mark given in an exam or for a piece of school work
words.exampleEn: (British English), She got good grades in her exams.

words.definitionEn: a person who has a university degree
words.exampleEn: job prospects for graduates

words.definitionEn: to get a degree, especially your first degree, from a university or college
words.exampleEn: She taught in France after she graduated.

words.definitionEn: the small hard seeds of food plants such as wheat, rice, etc.; a single seed of such a plant
words.exampleEn: Russia sold 12 million tons of grain abroad last year.

words.definitionEn: to tell people something officially, especially about a decision, plans, etc.
words.exampleEn: announce something, He officially announced his intention to resign at today's press conference.

words.definitionEn: feeling or showing thanks because somebody has done something kind for you or has done as you asked
words.exampleEn: Thank you for doing this. I really am so grateful.

words.definitionEn: a spoken or written statement that informs people about something
words.exampleEn: to make an announcement

words.definitionEn: to make somebody slightly angry
words.exampleEn: annoy somebody, His constant joking was beginning to annoy her.

words.definitionEn: the process in people, animals or plants of growing physically, mentally or emotionally
words.exampleEn: Lack of water will stunt the plant's growth.

words.definitionEn: slightly angry
words.exampleEn: annoyed with somebody at/about something, He was beginning to get very annoyed with me about my carelessness.

words.definitionEn: a person, such as a soldier, a police officer or a prison officer, who protects a place or people, or prevents prisoners from escaping
words.exampleEn: prison/border guards

words.definitionEn: to protect property, places or people from attack or danger
words.exampleEn: Armed officers guarded the entrance.

words.definitionEn: making somebody feel slightly angry
words.exampleEn: This interruption is very annoying.

words.definitionEn: feeling ashamed because you have done something that you know is wrong or have not done something that you should have done
words.exampleEn: Matt and Chrissy both looked equally guilty.

words.definitionEn: to pass or give something to somebody
words.exampleEn: hand something to somebody, She handed the letter to me.

words.definitionEn: to attach something, or to be attached, at the top so that the lower part is free or loose
words.exampleEn: hang something + adv./prep., Hang your coat on the hook.

words.definitionEn: the state of feeling or showing pleasure
words.exampleEn: to find true happiness

words.definitionEn: almost no; almost not; almost none
words.exampleEn: There's hardly any tea left.

words.definitionEn: a very strong feeling of dislike for somebody
words.exampleEn: She gave him a look of real hate.

words.definitionEn: to move in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: Where are we heading?

words.definitionEn: the title of a news article printed in large letters, especially at the top of the front page on a newspaper or the home page on a news website
words.exampleEn: a newspaper headline

words.definitionEn: the process of supplying heat to a room or building; a system used to do this
words.exampleEn: to put/turn the heating on

words.definitionEn: to a great degree; in large amounts
words.exampleEn: It was raining heavily.

words.definitionEn: an aircraft without wings that has large blades (= long flat parts) on top that go round. It can fly straight up from the ground and can also stay in one position in the air.
words.exampleEn: a police/rescue helicopter

words.definitionEn: the best, most interesting or most exciting part of something
words.exampleEn: One of the highlights of the trip was seeing the Taj Mahal.

words.definitionEn: to emphasize something, especially so that people give it more attention
words.exampleEn: The report highlighted the importance of exercise to maintain a healthy body.

words.definitionEn: very
words.exampleEn: It is highly unlikely that she'll be late.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody a job
words.exampleEn: She was hired three years ago.

words.definitionEn: important in history; likely to be thought of as important at some time in the future
words.exampleEn: the restoration of historic buildings

words.definitionEn: connected with the past
words.exampleEn: You must place these events in their historical context.

words.definitionEn: always telling the truth, and never stealing or cheating
words.exampleEn: an honest man/woman

words.definitionEn: very bad or unpleasant; used to describe something that you do not like
words.exampleEn: What horrible weather!

words.definitionEn: a feeling of great shock or fear
words.exampleEn: The thought of being left alone filled her with horror.

words.definitionEn: a person who invites guests to a meal, a party, etc. or who has people staying at their house
words.exampleEn: Ian, our host, introduced us to the other guests.

words.definitionEn: to go after wild animals in order to catch or kill them for food, sport or to make money
words.exampleEn: Lions sometimes hunt alone.

words.definitionEn: a violent storm with very strong winds, especially in the western Atlantic Ocean
words.exampleEn: A powerful hurricane hit the Florida coast.

words.definitionEn: the need or wish to get something done quickly
words.exampleEn: Take your time—there's no hurry.

words.definitionEn: to move or act quickly because there is not much time
words.exampleEn: You'll have to hurry if you want to catch that train.

words.definitionEn: who or what somebody/something is
words.exampleEn: The police are trying to discover the identity of the killer.

words.definitionEn: to pay no attention to something
words.exampleEn: He ignored all the ‘No Smoking’ signs and lit up a cigarette.

words.definitionEn: separated by a distance, of space or time
words.exampleEn: The two houses stood 500 metres apart.

words.definitionEn: not allowed by the law
words.exampleEn: illegal drugs/firearms/substances

words.definitionEn: existing only in your mind or imagination
words.exampleEn: The equator is an imaginary line around the middle of the earth.
